reviews.review_msg: I have had Scott for BUS330, BUS321, and BUS426; in fact, Scott has seriously exceeded my expectations in regards to teaching. He is accessible outside of class, enthusiastic, and is fair when it comes to examinations. Scott is clearly very knowledgable about the topics and genuinely cares about his students.... and honestly, he's kind of cute too

had for 321 and 426. Grant is one of my favourite professors at SFU. He explains everything really easily, and engages the class really well. He is very straightforward regarding expectations and is genuinely a very nice and relaxed guy.

Chill guy. Great at simplifying concepts + always ready to answer your questions.

1 midterm and 1 final (both non cumulative); rest of grades is online assignments.

Overall, can't complain, he's a really good professor.

Tests are fair; combination of theory and calculations.
Literally does not provide any accommodations, worst prof! Avoid!!

Taking him for 321. He is a great professor, the workload is quite light (midterm, final, and weekly assignments that take less than half an hour). He explains everything in simple terms, and provides practice problems that are similar to exam questions. Definetely recommend!
Preparation is key!

He seems to be a nice person. However he doesn't spend any time to explain things well. He rushes through lectures and finishes in 1-2 hours. This is supposed to be a 3 hour class! His lectures are pretty much useless as he simply reads off the slides. I recommend taking this class with someone else.

michael is an amazing prof. I can understand why people say hes hard but I really enjoy the real-life stories he shares and I found real value with the assignments we did in class especially with technology playing a bigger role now than ever before. Overall, I feel that I truly learnt something from his class. would take his classes again

He is a very mean professor. He talked so fast in class which you will not have time to take any notes. He did not give out any powerpoint slide before and most importantly you have to read the textbook. He wont give any half/ partial marks if your answer make sense but no quite really the sample answer. Not caring professor.

so easy to get distracted in lecture, prof spend half an hour talking thing that doesn't relate to class lol. be prepared to read every single word in the book and understand concepts in the corner of the book. exams grading is ridiculous... even try hard to explain a question, while, you have single variation with the "right" opinion, you get 0

To be honest, lectures were not very useful. I found Favere gets side tracked way too often and time is disproportionately spent on things that are of no value. His MCQ questions on the midterm and final are vague and his power point slides do a poor job explaining the concepts. Using more models and providing practice questions would help more.

He is definitely knowlegeable in audit field. He talks about lots of real life examples during lectures. We did weekly quizzes which you have to score 4 out of 4 in order to get one bonus mark. This quizzzes are nearly impossible to score perfect cuz you basically lack time. Midterm and final were fair but the case project was tough.

Worst accounting professor I have ever had. Takes 3 hours to complete a slide deck because he spends the majority of lecture talking about himself and his accomplishments to feed his ego. Throws curveball questions on the final exam. He also discriminates against non native English speakers, so beware.
My advice would be to not take BUS 426 with him unless you are capable of memorizing a 600 page dense and confusing textbook. Prof is straight up lazy.

I wouldn't recommend him!!!! Exams are really arbitrary, in terms of marking scheme. He prepare lecture notes (ppt) using another textbook, which he think is the best. His lecture notes ignores the real textbook, but test on the real textbook. In another word, his in-class teaching and exams do not match. Understanding and hard working do not help.
Not bad.  Exams were fair.

No Powerpoint slides, No course outline, unfinishable exam, never return any assignments (if ever marked), Weekly group cases. BUT one thing I admire him... Absolutely a skillful anti-consciousness robot dedicated in suffocating students with sleep pills while showing off his education and accomplishments for the purpose of indulging himself.

Class in summer = ridiculous. Mark based on 10% particip, 40%Hwk + 50%final. Hwk consist of few text Qs + he collects 1 Q, marks on effort. No motivation to try! He Would give the "correct" answer to only like 1/3 of the Qs. Particip is worse. Goes around the room asking for people's answers. Stupid. Final was unreasonable given what he 'taught'.

Since all students will get nearly the same mark for assignments and participation, the final exam is what will determine your mark.  Final was so incredibly hard, I spent the last 5 minutes wondering if I have to retake this nightmare course again.

Not a good professor/instructor. Lectures are ok, but could learn as much from the textbook. Exams are horrible (studying doesn't help) and grading is ridiculous. I got 60% on exam and A in the course - go figure...Maybe he's a good researcher, but I don't really care...
The final exam is horrible. You don't need to study hard for it...No matter how hard you try, you still screw up in his exam.

TERRIBLE TEACHER! More concerned with his research and EGO than anything else let alone teaching.  Got an A in his class, but no thanks to him.  Did not attend lectures after midterm because he accused me and one other student of cheating.

Absolute the worse instructor.  He is so high on himself and his research in "Audit", that he couldn't care less about the students.  He just regurgitates the text book and makes class attendance mandatory(probably just to waste our time). Avoid
hard exam.. and not helpful to students

Praise is an engaging prof! She always tries to go above and beyond to create fun lectures. The content is the difficult part. There is a lot to memorize and the audit process is quite complicated. As such, the prof and (dry) textbook are your best friends. Exams are reasonable; MC are a bit tricky, long answers very similar to in-class activities.

Hands down the best professor I've ever had at SFU. Explained concepts well during lectures and went the extra mile with slides and in-class activities to facilitate engagement (Kahoot, in-class problems). If you take BUS 426 (Audit), I highly recommend taking her class.
He is a pretty good professor, know what he is talking about. BUT, make sure you do read the text book and his notes...

Terrible lecturer, just repeats everything in the text. Exams are not really fair as he tests u on auditing applications, which he doesn't cover much. You'll have to do a lot of self-studying for this course.
Good prof and good class, but be sure to bring something along to lectures to keep yourself awake.
Not for ESL students.

Very ambiguous way of teaching. Asks you very obscure questions on auditing and then penalize you for not having the same answers that he had though. Multiple choice questions are ill prepared and lack objectivity. Overall not a good teacher.
Did not like his teaching at all. Only This guy has the ability to take a relatively easy course and make it exorbitantly hard to pass.

The most subjective marker I have ever had at SFU. Will give you a grade based on what mood he's in that day. Assignment/group project criteria is unclear, and he will give you a mark on the group project without telling you why you got that mark. If you have experience in audit/accounting, you'll do well cause the exam Qs are open-ended.

Grading criteria isn't clear at all. Multiple people from my class had emailed him weeks in advance to the final and he provided no clarification whatsoever. No practice problems were assigned throughout the semester, and he refused to provide any when asked. This made studying for the final even more difficult due to practical inexperience.
Passionate about course, overall a nice guy
He is not a good professor. He is vague about the grading skeeme. Avoid him.
He does not give clear guidelines as to what to expect in the exam. Overall not a good prof.

Dana is an expert in the field of auditing. He is very approachable and cares about all his students. He does an excellent job helping students learn about the basic framework and principles of auditing. Midterm (memorization) and final (application) were different.  Would be helpful if he used a mic. And written report requirements were unclear.

Professor Grieg is a very knowledgeable and passionate  individual about the subjects he teaches. With years of real world business experience he was able to bring the subject to life and give unique scenarios/cases of how the course was relevant. As a class of future CPA's we were all able to gain a healthy respect for being ethical & professional
Nice guy. He likes teaching and it shows.

Dana is the best professors I've had at SFU. He keeps his lectures interesting by sharing his field experience and talking about real-life challenges that the students might experience in their future careers. His exams and assignments are not easy, but he prepares the students very well for the challenges. Just follow his advice and you'll do well

He's a very good prof and tries to explain everything clearly. What I liked most about his class is that he doesn't give exams based on dead theory/memory-based questions. Instead, he gives you cases and you are expected to answer accordingly. Very useful if you plan on taking CASB. One of the best in SFU!

Anne is possibly one of the best profs that SFU has to offer. She is extremely knowledgeable with the course material and does whatever it takes to ensure that her students succeed in her class. You should definitely take a course with her if you have the opportunity!
I took Bus 251 and 426 with her. She's THE reason why it's worth studying accounting at SFU. I regret not taking 420 with her.

Her auditing class is amazing. Yes material is dull but she explains concepts using cases rather than textbook. Only need to read certain chapters (just read the important ones) and all the cases and slides for exams. No memo on my mt. Wish she taught all my acct courses
